steps.title: Test inequality I at P(3, -5)
steps.content:
Substitute into $y \lt -3x + 7$:
$$-5 \lt -3(3) + 7 = -9 + 7 = -2$$
Since $-5 \lt -2$ is true, inequality I holds.

steps.title: Test inequality II at P(3, -5)
steps.content:
Substitute into $y \gt x - 5$:
$$-5 \gt 3 - 5 = -2$$
Since $-5 \gt -2$ is false, inequality II does not hold.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• A. I only — I is true and II is false; matches.
  • B. II only — II is false, so this is incorrect.
  • C. I and II — II fails, so this is incorrect.
  • D. Neither — I holds, so this is incorrect.
A is correct.
